2604.06391.title: Toward a universal foundation model for graph-structured data
2604.06391.paper_summary: Graphs are a central representation in biomedical research, capturing molecular interaction networks, gene regulatory circuits, cell--cell communication maps, and knowledge graphs. Despite their importance, currently there is not a broadly reusable foundation model available for graph analysis comparable to those that have transformed language and vision. Existing graph neural networks are typically trained on a single dataset and learn representations specific only to that graph's node features, topology, and label space, limiting their ability to transfer across domains. This lack of generalization is particularly problematic in biology and medicine, where networks vary substantially across cohorts, assays, and institutions. Here we introduce a graph foundation model designed to learn transferable structural representations that are not specific to specific node identities or feature schemes. Our approach leverages feature-agnostic graph properties, including degree statistics, centrality measures, community structure indicators, and diffusion-based signatures, and encodes them as structural prompts. These prompts are integrated with a message-passing backbone to embed diverse graphs into a shared representation space. The model is pretrained once on heterogeneous graphs and subsequently reused on unseen datasets with minimal adaptation. Across multiple benchmarks, our pretrained model matches or exceeds strong supervised baselines while demonstrating superior zero-shot and few-shot generalization on held-out graphs. On the SagePPI benchmark, supervised fine-tuning of the pretrained backbone achieves a mean ROC-AUC of 95.5%, a gain of 21.8% over the best supervised message-passing baseline. The proposed technique thus provides a unique approach toward reusable, foundation-scale models for graph-structured data in biomedical and network science applications.
2604.06391.paper_summary_zh: 圖形是生物醫學研究中的核心表現，捕捉分子互動網絡、基因調控電路、細胞間通信地圖和知識圖譜。儘管它們的重要性，目前尚未有一個可廣泛重用的基礎模型可用於圖形分析，這與那些已經改變語言和視覺的模型相比。現有的圖神經網絡通常是在單一數據集上訓練的，僅學習該圖的節點特徵、拓撲和標籤空間的特定表示，限制了它們在不同領域之間的轉移能力。這種缺乏泛化的情況在生物學和醫學中尤其成為問題，因為網絡在不同的隊列、檢測和機構之間變化顯著。在此，我們介紹了一種圖形基礎模型，旨在學習可轉移的結構表示，這些表示並不特定於特定的節點身份或特徵方案。我們的方法利用無關特徵的圖形屬性，包括度數統計、中心性度量、社區結構指標和基於擴散的特徵，並將它們編碼為結構提示。這些提示與消息傳遞背骨集成，以將多樣的圖形嵌入到共享的表示空間中。該模型在異質圖形上進行了一次預訓練，隨後在未見數據集上以最小的適應性重用。在多個基準測試中，我們的預訓練模型達到或超過強大的監督基準，同時在保留圖形上展示出優越的零-shot和少-shot泛化能力。在SagePPI基準上，對預訓練背骨的監督微調達到95.5%的平均ROC-AUC，比最佳監督消息傳遞基準提高了21.8%。因此，所提出的技術為生物醫學和網絡科學應用中的圖形結構數據提供了一種獨特的可重用的基礎規模模型的方法。
2604.06391.author: Sakib Mostafa et.al.
2604.06391.authors: Sakib Mostafa, Lei Xing, Md. Tauhidul Islam
